  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Double-tapped circuit breakers that are not listed or identified for securing more than one conductor."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the extra conductor from each non-listed double-tapped breaker.",
    "Install a listed terminal block, pigtail the conductors, or replace the breakers with types listed for dual-conductor termination."
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC Section 110.14(A) states that terminals for more than one conductor shall be so identified. Standard circuit breakers are typically only listed for a single conductor unless explicitly marked otherwise by the manufacturer. Double-tapping non-listed breakers can lead to loose connections, arcing, and fire hazards.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.14(A)",
    "NEC Art. 408"
  ]
}
